articles.title: The low-caste hate thread dominated today
articles.body: Anon started a thread on low-caste hate, which unsurprisingly garnered a lot of attention. Several respondents chimed in, calling for the extermination of low castes. One respondent noted that low castes have ruined Indian institutions. Another thread that gained traction was about a gay erotic model's death. Anon wondered if low-caste genetics could explain why some men become 'cucks'. A thread on the 'Incel to Chad transformation' also popped up, with respondents offering dubious advice. The board's obsession with caste and social hierarchy continued.

articles.title: Daily low caste hate a thread
articles.body: Anon started the thread with a call to kill and dehead low castes. Responses included suggestions to drop low castes into acid and use them as manure. One respondent argued only reason India is still poor is because of low castes. Several demanded that low castes be stopped from using upper-caste surnames. The thread kept going with more calls to harm low castes. Anon wondered if this was an example of 'dysgenic' behavior. The threat to low castes was clear throughout. India does need to focus on stopping such attacks on low castes.

articles.title: The Dark Side of Erotic
articles.body: Anon shared a graphic scenario involving a 24-year-old Onlyfans model who died during a livestream. He was an erotic gay model. The discussion revolved around the circumstances of his death. A 27-year-old man had died similarly while streaming on LiveJasmin. These cases sparked debate on the sexual subcultures these models got involved in. Some threads questioned if there was a voyeuristic aspect to some models' deaths.

articles.title: The Incel to Chad transformation
articles.body: Anon asked how to go from being an incel to a 'chad' in two months. Responses ranged from exercise and eating protein to stopping masturbation. The thread had a mix of sarcastic and genuine advice. Some respondents claimed having a beard and good haircut could help. The low-caste background of some respondents likely factored into the advice given. Low castes are likely to get harsh responses on /b/.

articles.title: Site Shill Thread
articles.body: Anon started a thread to shill their website on Instagram and Reddit to get more traffic. Responses to the thread included various ideas on how to increase traffic. Some respondents mentioned they'd stop posting low-level design issues. A few emphasized not spamming scat or extreme content. Site mods got a reminder to keep trigger fingers in check.
